name: ACI Central (Philippine-Australian Arts, Culture and Innovation Central)
description:
ACI Central is a Filipino-Australian community hub in Campbelltown that brings together arts, culture, and innovation under one roof. Operated by the NARRA Co-operative, the centre serves as a meeting space, art gallery, and shared office for the local Filipino community and beyond.

The centre regularly hosts cultural showcases, art exhibitions, community fundraisers, and educational programmes. It provides a welcoming space where Filipino heritage is celebrated through music, visual arts, and cultural traditions. Local artists, performers, and entrepreneurs use the venue to connect, collaborate, and share their work with the wider Campbelltown community.

ACI Central also supports emerging talent through creative workshops and vocal coaching sessions. The space has partnered with organisations including the Philippine Consulate General in Sydney to run community engagement programmes and support initiatives for international students.

Located at Shop 4, 3-9 Warby Street in the heart of Campbelltown, the centre is easily accessible and open to everyone. Whether you are looking for a cultural event to attend, a creative space to use, or a community to join, ACI Central offers a genuine connection to Filipino-Australian culture in south-western Sydney.
